vitals.terrain: Rolling hills and flat stages through Southland region
vitals.field_size: Teams and individual riders, typically 100+ participants
vitals.participation_trend: Stable, with growth as NZ's premier multi-day stage race

course_description.overview: Multi-day road stage race covering approximately 800 km through Southland's deep south, featuring 7-8 stages with circuit adjustments for 2026 starting and finishing at Waikiwi Rugby Club on Bainfield Road. Includes challenging weather-exposed terrain typical of the region.[1][3][5][6]

key_segments.name: Stage 8: Winton to Invercargill
key_segments.description: Final stage example from past events, flat sprint finish.[6]

key_segments.name: Invercargill Criterium
key_segments.description: Pre-tour NZ Criterium Championships on January 17 around Invercargill streets.[3][8]

key_segments.name: Multi-stage circuits
key_segments.description: Rolling Southland roads with local viewing points near homes and workplaces.[5]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Toughest and most prestigious multi-day stage race in New Zealand since 1956, known for extreme weather challenges like winds and snow, testing endurance over deep south terrain.[1][7]

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Invercargill Airport (INV); event based in Invercargill with stages across Southland region.
logistics.accommodation: Limited availability considered in scheduling; book early due to regional events and post-emergency recovery.[1]
logistics.registration_details: Via Cycling Southland or tourofsouthland.com; team-based entries for this elite NRS-level event.[7]
logistics.bike_logistics: Standard road bike; transport within Southland, support for teams provided.
logistics.local_considerations: Post-State of Emergency recovery in 2025; potential weather impacts (winds, snow); two editions in 2026.[1][3]

final_verdict.one_liner: New Zealand's iconic multi-day road stage race, toughest in the south with dual 2026 editions.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Elite road cyclists, teams, and NZ national development riders seeking prestige and tough conditions.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for serious racers chasing NRS points and Southland's legendary challenge.
pro_tips: Prepare for Southern weather extremes: wind, rain, possible snow.[1]
Enter as team for support; check Cycling Southland for 2026 regs.[7]
Catch the pre-tour Criterium Nats on Jan 17 for warm-up.[3]
